answer: Interstate 235 is located in Iowa, running just north of downtown Des Moines through the heart of the Des Moines metropolitan area from West Des Moines to Ankeny [RAM0032-D01]. I-235 is an auxiliary Interstate Highway that was completely rebuilt and widened during a project spanning most of the 2000s [RAM0032-D01].

answer: The Museum of Applied Arts was established in 1874 [RAM0172-D01]. Its building was constructed between 1893 and 1896 [RAM0172-D02], [RAM0172-D03].

claims.claim: The Museum of Applied Arts was founded in 1874 [RAM0172-D01]
Its building was constructed between 1893 and 1896 [RAM0172-D02], [RAM0172-D03]

primary_revision_trace.after: The Museum of Applied Arts was founded in 1874 [RAM0172-D01] as the "Kunstgewerbemuseum" (Museum of Arts and Crafts). It moved into its present location, the New Grassi Building, in 1926. Numerous exhibits were destroyed by bombs during World War II. Between 1981 and 1994, no permanent exhibition was possible because of damage to the building. The Grassi Museum was refurbished from 2001 to 2006. The Museum of Applied Arts received its present name in 2005, and the new permanent exhibition opened in 2007. answer: The artist of "David with the Head of Goliath" is Massimo Stanzione; specifically for the work dated c. 1630 exhibited at the San Diego Museum of Art [RAM0176-D05], while a separate version attributed to him is noted with dates ranging from c. 1642-1643 [RAM0176-D07]. Caravaggio also treated this subject in works dated c. 1607 and c. 1600, currently located in the Kunsthistorisches Museum in Vienna and the Prado in Madrid respectively [RAM0176-D01][RAM0176-D04], though Leonardo da Vinci is associated with a work cited as being dated c. 1607 which appears to be an error regarding Caravaggio's location or date based on conflicting text within the provided corpus [RAM0176-D03].

answer: The term "Siraya" refers to an Austronesian language spoken by indigenous communities in southwestern Taiwan; historically, the name was also associated with a type of ancient currency used for trade among these communities. Additionally, it designates the Sirayan people who are ethnically and linguistically Austronesian. While Taivoan and Makatao were formerly considered part of the Siraya group, modern linguistic discoveries classify them as independent peoples distinct from the Siraya group [RAM0270-D01][RAM0270-D03].

answer: The 487th refers to a provisional United States Air Force unit specifically designated as an Air Expeditionary Wing; its lineage includes inactive predecessors such as the 487th Bombardment Group (activated September 1943), the 487th Fighter Squadron (activated September 1942, last assigned with the 352d Fighter Group at Camp Kilmer, New Jersey), and the 487th Bombardment Squadron (established mid-1942 as a B-25 Mitchell medium bomber squadron, inactivated on 1 September 1963). answer: No. 94 Squadron was formed in **1917** [RAM0334-D05][RAM0334-D03], with subsequent formations occurring on May 30, 1945 [RAM0334-D02][RAM0334-D01] and March 26, 1952 [RAM0334-D06].

claims.claim: No
94 Squadron was formed on August 1, 1917 [RAM0334-D05][RAM0334-D03]
It has been formed a total of four times: the first formation occurred at RAF Harling Road in 1917
the second (No
94 Squadron RAAF) was formed on May 30, 1945 [RAM0334-D02][RAM0334-D01]
a third iteration existed from March 26, 1952 to September 15, 1957
and the fourth (VFA-94) was established on March 26, 1952 [RAM0334-D06][RAM0334-D04]

primary_revision_trace.after: No. 94 Squadron RAF was formed at Harling Road on 1 August 1917 as a training unit for the Sopwith Camel; it mobilised in May 1918, moved to France in October 1918, returned to the UK without seeing action due to the imminent end of World War I, and disbanded on 30 June 1919. The squadron was later reformed at Castlereagh, New South Wales, on 30 May 1945; it commenced training in July 1945 but did not complete its initial preparations before the end of World War II. Additionally, Fighter Squadron 94 (VF-94) was established at NAS Alameda, California, on March 26, 1952.

answer:
The feast day of Saint Marcellus varies by specific identity:

*   **Saint Marcellus of Capua:** Celebrated on **7 October** [RAM0470-D01], **[RAM0470-D03]**, and also listed as **15 January** in some sources [RAM0470-D04].
*   **Marcellus of Tangier (Saint Marcellus the Centurion):** Celebrated on **October 30** [RAM0470-D06].
*   **Pope Saint Marcellus I:** His feast day was observed on **16 January**, though this date corresponds to his death and end of papacy rather than a general commemoration in all calendars, with other sources noting Apuleius moved to 8 October [RAM0470-D05], **[RAM0470-D07]**.

claims.claim: The feast day for Saint Marcellus depends on which specific saint is referenced, as there are multiple saints named Marcellus mentioned in the provided text:
*   **Saint Marcellus of Capua:** His feast day is listed as **15 January** [RAM0470-D04] and also **7 October** (noting that Apuleius was removed from this date) [RAM0470-D03]
